lede: A look at October 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jared Tallent and Eminem, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Rehavam Ze'evi
events.desc: Israeli tourism minister Rehavam Ze'evi is assassinated by Hamdi Quran, a member of the PFLP, thus becoming the highest-ranking Israeli to be killed by a Palestinian.

events.title: Hatfield rail crash
events.desc: The Hatfield rail crash leads to the collapse of Railtrack.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The USS Kearny becomes the first U.S. Navy vessel to be torpedoed by a U-boat.

events.title: Albert Einstein
events.desc: Albert Einstein flees Nazi Germany and moves to the United States.

events.title: Al Capone
events.desc: Al Capone is convicted of income tax evasion.

events.title: The Open Championship
events.desc: First The Open Championship (referred to in North America as the British Open).

events.title: London Beer Flood
events.desc: Eight people die in the London Beer Flood.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British General John Burgoyne surrenders his army at Saratoga, New York.

events.title: Ascanio in Alba
events.desc: Premiere in Milan of the opera Ascanio in Alba, composed by Mozart at age 15.

events.title: List of regicides of Charles I of England
events.desc: The nine regicides who signed the death warrant of Charles I of England are hanged, drawn and quartered.

births.name: Josep Puig i Cadafalch
births.line: Catalan architect who designed the Casa Martí

deaths.text: Stanford R. Ovshinsky, American scientist and businessman, co-founded Energy Conversion Devices
Rehavam Ze'evi, Israeli historian, general, and politician, Tourism Minister of Israel
Giovanni Gronchi, Italian educator, soldier, and politician, 3rd President of the Italian Republic

observances.text: Christian feast day: François-Isidore Gagelin (one of Vietnamese Martyrs)
